tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57945,"title":"[ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Document redirect policy behavior and fill in Examples section","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` **Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] by `@ryazhang-microsoft` ## What Changed The commit cached the primary ACL node URL inside `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to speed up write operations. When the service issues a 307/308 redirect pointing to the primary node, the policy now stores that target and sends subsequent non-GET requests directly to it — bypassing the load balancer round-trip. The cache is invalidated on transport failures or 5xx responses so the client can re-discover the primary node automatically. ## Gaps Found - The README **Key concepts** section has no mention of the SDK's transparent redirect-following behavior (introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3, enhanced in 1.4.1-beta.5). Users who observe cross-node routing or performance differences have no documentation to explain what the SDK is doing automatically. - The redirect caching optimization — and its automatic invalidation on node failures — is not documented anywhere for end users. - The **Examples** section of the README contains only `Coming Soon...` with no working code examples at all. - The CHANGELOG captures all these changes but the README has not been brought up to date. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` **Action:** Edit 1. In the **Key concepts** section, add a new sub-section **Redirect and primary-node routing** after the existing sub-sections: ```markdown ### Redirect and primary-node routing Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system where write operations must be processed by the primary node. Non-primary nodes respond to write requests with an HTTP 307 or 308 redirect to the current primary. The SDK's built-in redirect policy (`ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`) handles these redirects transparently: - Redirects are followed automatically while preserving the `Authorization` header, which is required because ACL nodes are all trusted within the same ledger. - After the first successful redirect, the primary node's URL is cached inside the client. Subsequent write requests are sent directly to the cached primary, avoiding an extra round-trip through the load balancer. - The cache is automatically invalidated when a transport failure or a 5xx response is received from the cached primary, allowing the client to re-discover the primary node through the normal redirect flow. This behavior is transparent — no configuration is required. Users with write-heavy workloads benefit automatically from reduced latency once the primary node has been identified. ``` 2. Replace the **Examples** section content: ```markdown ## Examples The following examples demonstrate common operations with Azure Confidential Ledger. ### Append a ledger entry ``` Operation postOperation = ledgerClient.PostLedgerEntry( waitUntil: WaitUntil.Completed, RequestContent.Create( new { contents = "Hello world!" })); string transactionId = postOperation.Id; Console.WriteLine($"Appended transaction with Id: {transactionId}"); ``` ### Get transaction status ``` Response statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); string status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); // Wait for the entry to be committed while (status == "Pending") { statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); } Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); ``` ### Get a receipt for a transaction ``` Response receiptResponse = ledgerClient.GetReceipt(transactionId); string receiptJson = new StreamReader(receiptResponse.ContentStream).ReadToEnd(); Console.WriteLine(receiptJson); ``` ``` > **Note:** The snippet placeholders (`Snippet:AppendToLedger`, `Snippet:GetStatus`, `Snippet:GetReceipt`) already exist in the existing README so they do not need to be added — but an explicit **Examples** intro and section headings should be added to give each snippet meaningful context. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The existing README already references snippet regions `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, and `GetReceipt` that are backed by test files. No new snippet regions need to be created for this documentation update. However, if a new snippet demonstrating the redirect behavior is desired, the following approach should be used: 1. **Test file:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/` — create or extend an existing sample class with a method that performs a write operation; the redirect policy behavior is automatic. 2. **README placeholder:** Add `` ```C# Snippet: `` followed by a closing `` ``` `` at the desired location in the README. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The current README contains these required sections but some are incomplete: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started | ✅ Present and populated | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing redirect/primary-node routing sub-section | | Examples | ❌ Empty ("Coming Soon...") | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` 5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24155040683/agentic_workflow) · ● 348.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57945","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T19:51:15Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T19:51:15Z"}

No new snippet regions need to be created for this documentation update. However, if a new snippet demonstrating the redirect behavior is desired, the following approach should be used: 1. **Test file:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/` — create or extend an existing sample class with a method that performs a write operation; the redirect policy behavior is automatic. 2. **README placeholder:** Add `` ```C# Snippet: `` followed by a closing `` ``` `` at the desired location in the README. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The current README contains these required sections but some are incomplete: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started | ✅ Present and populated | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing redirect/primary-node routing sub-section | | Examples | ❌ Empty ("Coming Soon...") | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` 5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` ## arguments.owners: andpiccione, Azure/azure-sdk-write-acl, ivarprudnikov, msftsettiy, musabbir, PallabPaul, ryazhang-microsoft, weirenlai

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` README — specifically, the missing documentation for the SDK's redirect/primary-node caching behavior (introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3/1.4.1-beta.5) and an empty Examples section that still shows "Coming Soon...".

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` (latest beta: 1.4.1-beta.5)
**Affected API:** `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` (redirect caching behavior)
**Scenarios:**
- No documentation in README Key Concepts explaining transparent 307/308 redirect handling and primary-node URL caching
- No documentation of automatic cache invalidation on transport failures or 5xx responses
- Examples section is entirely empty ("Coming Soon...") with no working code samples

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` to add a "Redirect and primary-node routing" sub-section under Key Concepts and populate the Examples section with working code.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue. The implementation already exists (committed in [REDACTED]); only the README needs to be updated to reflect it.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` source to confirm caching behavior matches proposed documentation
2. Check `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` current Examples section for the "Coming Soon..." placeholder
3. Verify snippet region names (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`) in test/sample files to ensure code examples are accurate
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` after README changes to validate snippet references
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — Package is `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` (not `Azure.ResourceManager.*`), so this is a data-plane client library
- **Service:** `Confidential Ledger` — Issue explicitly names the service directory `sdk/confidentialledger/` and package `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger`
- **Confidence:** High — The package and service are unambiguously named in the issue body; category is clearly Client (not Mgmt or Provisioning)
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Confidential Ledger` (line 250) — matched because issue labels include "Confidential Ledger" and the entry requires only that label
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @andpiccione @Azure/azure-sdk-write-acl @ivarprudnikov @msftsettiy @musabbir @PallabPaul @ryazhang-microsoft @weirenlai
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; left issue unassigned (no AzureSdkOwners); issue is bot-filed so "needs-team-attention" was not added
- **Scan notes:** During bottom-to-top scan, `# ServiceLabel: %Confidential Ledger %Mgmt` (line 960) was encountered first but skipped — requires "Mgmt" which is not in this issue's labels. The less-specific `%Confidential Ledger`-only entry at line 250 was the first full match.
</details>
